intro: LottieFiles and Feather Icons diverge first on pricing model: LottieFiles uses freemium pricing with a free tier, Feather Icons uses free pricing with a free tier. Everything else flows from that commercial choice. LottieFiles is built for design and product teams integrating lottie animations across web and mobile apps and is not a fit for solo designers needing only a lightweight web editor or teams avoiding subscription tools. Feather Icons is built for legacy projects already using feather or fans of the minimalist single-weight style and is not a fit for new projects needing active updates, more icons, or multiple weights. On the scoring side, LottieFiles rates 5/5 for developer experience and 3/5 for transparency; Feather Icons rates 4/5 and 5/5 respectively. The honest trade-off: with LottieFiles you accept that pricing has crept up for team plans, while Feather Icons comes with the reality that maintenance has been slow for years. Neither side is free.
quick_take: LottieFiles is for design and product teams integrating lottie animations across web and mobile apps; Feather Icons is for legacy projects already using feather or fans of the minimalist single-weight.
verdict_a: Choose LottieFiles if you are design and product teams integrating lottie animations across web and mobile apps and a freemium pricing model fits how you buy.
verdict_b: Choose Feather Icons if you are legacy projects already using feather or fans of the minimalist single-weight style and a free pricing model fits how you buy.

faq.q: Is LottieFiles cheaper than Feather Icons?
faq.a: LottieFiles uses a freemium model, while Feather Icons uses a free model, and both publish a free tier. We don't have per-seat numbers in this dataset, so 'cheaper' depends on volume. Price both on your actual usage before deciding.

faq.q: Can I migrate from LottieFiles to Feather Icons?
faq.a: LottieFiles has low lock-in (lottie json open format); Feather Icons has low lock-in (oss svg icon set). Migration effort scales with how much state and automation you've built in LottieFiles; plan for data export, config rebuild, and a parallel-run period before cutting over.

In our data, LottieFiles scores 5/5 for developer experience versus 4/5 for Feather Icons. That said, DX is subjective; the Feather Icons score may still be acceptable if its feature fit is stronger for your use case.

faq.q: Is Feather Icons a good alternative to LottieFiles?
faq.a: Feather Icons is built for legacy projects already using feather or fans of the minimalist single-weight style and explicitly not for new projects needing active updates, more icons, or multiple weights, so it's a fit only if your workflow matches its stated audience. If you were using LottieFiles for design and product teams integrating lottie animations across web and mobile apps, the switch is reasonable; outside that scope, look elsewhere.
